evals.prompt: Use $repair-pr to address the actionable comments on the current PR.
expectations: Resolves the PR and reads review comments, thread state, and checks before editing.
Clusters actionable in-scope feedback separately from questions, outdated comments, and product judgment.
Patches only in-scope issues, runs targeted validation, commits, and pushes repair commits.
Resolves or replies only to threads clearly mapped to fixes made in this run.

evals.prompt: Use $repair-pr to fix the PR's failing CI job.
expectations: Inspects the failing check or logs enough to decide whether the failure belongs to this PR.
Applies a scoped fix only when the failure is same-story.
Runs the relevant local validation before committing.
Does not turn unrelated or flaky CI into a broad refactor.

evals.prompt: Use $repair-pr on this PR. The reviewer is asking whether we should change the product behavior.
expectations: Recognizes the comment as product judgment or scope expansion.
Asks the user for the decision instead of choosing behavior silently.
Does not push a speculative product change.
Reports what can be fixed without that decision, if anything.

evals.prompt: Use $repair-pr to resolve all review threads after the latest push.
expectations: Maps each thread to an actual fix or explanation before resolving it.
Leaves unmapped, stale, conflicting, or judgment-heavy threads unresolved.
Uses GraphQL resolution only for threads addressed in this run.
Reports remaining unresolved threads and why they were left open.
